step.title: Step 38
step.actionDisplay: MOUSE(row=55, col=25) -> MOUSE(row=55, col=35) -> MOUSE(row=60, col=30)

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
All tiles are now gray (g) again. The board has reset! The slider moved from cols 39-40 to cols 13-16 (left end). So clicking (60,30) when NOT all 3 required tiles are set correctly resets the board.
But wait - I had (50,30) set to green before clicking (55,25), (55,35), and (60,30). So the state before clicking was:
- (50,30) = green
- (55,25) = white → clicked → green
- (55,35) = white → clicked → green
- (60,30) = white → clicked → ???
The result is ALL gray. So clicking (60,30) reset everything. But I had 3 green tiles (50,30, 55,25, 55,35) before clicking (60,30). This should have been the correct pattern!
Wait, but the tiles were in a MIXED state: (50,30) was green from before, and (55,25), (55,35) were just turned from white to green in this batch. Maybe the issue is that all 4 actions happened in one batch, so the game saw them all at once and treated (60,30) as a reset.
Let me try a different approach:
1. First set the 3 tiles to green in one batch
2. THEN click (60,30) in a SEPARATE action
But first I need to make sure the board is reset properly. Currently all tiles are gray, which is the starting state. The slider is at the left end. Good.
Now let me:
1. Click (50,30), (55,25), (55,35) to set them green
2. In a SEPARATE action, click (60,30) to submit

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Board is reset: all tiles gray, slider at left
# New plan: set 3 tiles green first, THEN click (60,30) separately

# Step 1: Set the 3 tiles
result1 = action([
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 50, 'col': 30},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 25},
    {'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 35}
])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
tiles = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['boundary']:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        cr, cc = (min(rows)+max(rows))//2, (min(cols)+max(cols))//2
        if 49 <= cr <= 61 and 24 <= cc <= 36 and n['pixels'] == 9:
            tiles[(cr,cc)] = n['color']
print("After setting 3 tiles:", tiles)

# Step 2: Click (60,30) to submit
result2 = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 60, 'col': 30}])

seg = current_frame.segmentation
tiles = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['boundary']:
        rows = [p[0] for p in n['boundary']]
        cols = [p[1] for p in n['boundary']]
        cr, cc = (min(rows)+max(rows))//2, (min(cols)+max(cols))//2
        if 49 <= cr <= 61 and 24 <= cc <= 36 and n['pixels'] == 9:
            tiles[(cr,cc)] = n['color']
print("After clicking (60,30):", tiles)
print("Level completed:", last_action_result.get('level_completed'))
print("Game over:", last_action_result.get('game_over'))
print("Done:", last_action_result.get('done'))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
